Refactor · Migration One-way migration motivated by a collision bug

Runtime tracking layout

Namespace the .cast runtime link per goal

Turn the per-directory singleton <ext>/.cast symlink into a directory of per-goal links <ext>/.cast/<slug>, so two goals that share one external project directory stop silently misrouting each other's runtime writes — migrating any existing bare-symlink layout in place.

Key outcome

Shared external dirs become safe — each goal resolves its own .cast/<slug> instead of a single link they fight over.

Scope fence

Only the link layout under the external repo changes; where runtime directories actually live goals_dir/<slug> does not move.

01

Why we're changing the shape

A single shared .cast link can only point one place at a time — so when two goals share an external dir, dispatching one repoints it away from the other and that goal's writes land in the wrong place with no error at all.

Today — singleton link

One .cast symlink, recreated on every dispatch, can name only a single goal's runtime directory.

<ext>/.cast → goals_dir/goal-A
# goal-B dispatched →
<ext>/.cast → goals_dir/goal-B

Silent misroute: goal-A's runtime writes now follow .cast to goal-B's directory. No exception, no warning — just data in the wrong place.

→

After — per-goal namespace

A .cast directory holds one link per goal, so both coexist under the same external repo.

<ext>/.cast/goal-A → goals_dir/goal-A
<ext>/.cast/goal-B → goals_dir/goal-B

Structural fix: the singleton becomes a namespace. Because older checkouts already wrote the bare-link layout, this is a one-way migration that carries the legacy case forward rather than assuming a clean slate.

02

The change we're making

Three committed decisions: the layout flips to a per-goal directory, removal becomes per-goal and self-cleaning, and dispatch resolves through the namespace.

1

.cast becomes a directory of per-goal symlinks core decision

ensure_cast_symlink writes <ext>/.cast/<slug> → goals_dir/<slug>, and on first touch migrates a legacy bare .cast symlink into the new directory form.

2

Removal is per-goal and self-cleaning

remove_cast_symlink takes a slug, removes only that goal's link, and deletes .cast only once it is empty. Both update_config callers are updated to pass the slug.

3

Dispatch resolves through the namespace

agent_service dispatch and invoke tracking directories now resolve to .cast/<slug> rather than the bare link.

03

Deliberately out of scope

Only the link layout under the external repo moves — the runtime directories, the dispatch/monitoring contract, and any eager backfill all stay put.

  • × Where runtime directories live. goals_dir/<slug> is untouched — only the link layout under the external repo changes.
  • × The dispatch / monitoring contract. Nothing changes beyond the path the tracking directory now resolves to.
  • × Eager backfill of links. Goals never dispatched into the shared repo get no link — links are still created on dispatch, not ahead of time.
04

Still open

One unresolved question — left open, not decided.

Open question

When a .cast/<slug> link is orphaned because its goal was deleted out-of-band, should it be garbage-collected on the next dispatch — or left until the empty-directory cleanup eventually removes the parent .cast?

  • AGarbage-collect the orphaned link proactively on the next dispatch.
  • BLeave it; let empty-directory cleanup reclaim the parent when nothing is left.
Refactor · Migration — one-way goal: family-refactor-migration
